title: STAT+: HHS convenes health care execs, motivational speaker on panel to modernize health care
summary: The members will advise Kennedy and Oz on how to lower costs and improve care.

title: Eli Lilly’s deal man, Allogene’s off-the-shelf CAR-T, and Merck’s Terns buy
summary: This week on "The Readout LOUD" podcast: Eli Lilly's deal man, Allogene's off-the-shelf CAR-T, and Merck's Terns buy.

title: Merck’s deal for Terns sparks debate over a possible biotech bidding war
summary: Some investors and analysts believe the pharma’s $6.7 billion offer is justified given Terns’ recent stock run. Others are convinced a better proposal is coming.

title: STAT+: Denali Therapeutics, Corcept Therapeutics win FDA approvals
summary: In today's Readout Newsletter, Denali and Corcept win FDA approvals, Sarepta eyes a rebound, and Allogene progresses with an off-the-shelf CAR-T.

title: Health influencers talk social media and mental health
summary: In this week's STATus Report, influential health communicators Jessica Knurick, Morgan “Dr. Noc” McSweeney, and Jessica Malaty Rivera chat about the mental health impacts of social media.

title: FDA clears Denali drug in ‘clear step’ for rare disease biotechs
summary: The approval of Denali’s Hunter syndrome treatment, Avlayah, comes after a series of drug rejections and delays that had led to criticism of the FDA’s stance on rare disease therapies.

title: STAT+: A surprise opening for health care reforms
summary: GOP moves to resolve the fight over ICE's funding could prompt more changes to health care

title: Wave crashes on obesity drug update; Kodiak’s reboot pays dividends
summary: Wave’s drug, which is meant to improve body composition, disappointed investors but was defended by analysts. Elsewhere, Kodiak’s revived vision loss treatment succeeded in a second Phase 3 trial.

title: White House misses deadline to nominate new CDC director
summary: In today's Morning Rounds newsletter: White House misses the deadline to nominate a new CDC director, California furthers its crackdown on ultra-processed foods, and more.

title: STAT+: Data show people racing to chatbots for health advice
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: More people use chatbots for health advice, a health AI startup raises $125 million, and all about deepfaked medical images.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about an FDA OK for a rare disease drug, a grilling of the PTO chief, and more
summary: The FDA approved a Denali medicine for Hunter syndrome, a notable decision as the agency has recently taken tougher stances on rare disease drugs

title: STAT+: Allogene’s off-the-shelf CAR-T is nearing an early but pivotal study readout
summary: A preliminary readout from a study could vindicate Allogene Therapeutics' stubborn belief in its off-the-shelf CAR-T treatment.

title: Opinion: Endometriosis is not just a gynecological disease
summary: Endometriosis is not just a gynecological disease.

title: Opinion: The perimenopause movement sells women the lie that they are ruled by their hormones
summary: The recent explosion of perimenopause attention tells women that they are controlled by their hormones.

title: Oral macrocyclic IL-23 inhibitor nabs FDA approval for psoriasis
STAT+: CDC leadership in limbo as Trump administration set to miss deadline to nominate director
summary: RFK Jr. and a top adviser are continuing the search for a new CDC director past a statutory deadline.

title: Maze tumbles despite positive data for kidney disease drug
summary: Investors had reservations about data for a drug that could compete with a candidate from Vertex in APOL1-mediated kidney disease.

title: California considers seal of approval for foods that are not ultra-processed
summary: A California bill would allow "not ultraprocessed" labels on foods, even as scientists debate definitions and courts question the legality of similar laws.

title: STAT+: Bhattacharya addresses CDC director role, works to bolster staff morale in first all-hands meeting
summary: Bhattacharya faced tough staff questions on job cuts and fallout from the shooting attack on the agency.

title: Heart failure is common. A quick MRI could improve diagnosis and guide treatment
summary: A new MRI method measures cardiac oxygen use in three minutes without catheters or radiation, offering a simpler way to study heart failure and metabolism.

title: Beam posts positive data on base editing treatment for AATD
summary: Updated data showed the biotech's approach to the rare lung and liver condition helped restore functional protective proteins in a small study.

title: STAT+: FDA approves Denali Therapeutics drug for Hunter syndrome
summary: The FDA approved a new medicine from Denali Therapeutics for Hunter syndrome, a notable decision by the agency after a string of rejections of rare disease drugs.

title: STAT+: A 2023 health AI bet that seems to be paying off
summary: In this edition of STAT's AI Prognosis: A chat with Qualified Health co-founder and CEO, Justin Norden, as the company raises $125 million.

title: FDA Approves Drug to Treat Neurologic Manifestations of Hunter Syndrome
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Avlayah (tividenofusp alfa-eknm) to treat certain individuals with Hunter syndrome (Mucopolysaccharidosis type II or MPS II).

title: Sarepta sees early success with RNAi drugs from Arrowhead
summary: Long-awaited results from Phase 1/2 studies lifted Sarepta’s stock and, according to the company, suggest potential for two medicines to treat muscle-weakening conditions.

title: STAT+: Sarepta Therapeutics shares rise on early promise for rare disease drugs
summary: Sarepta Therapeutics’ next crop of medicines for muscular dystrophy appear to be safe and effective in early clinical trials, the company said.

summary: Eli Lilly and the "societal obligation" of GLP-1s, Merck's Terns Pharma purchase, and more biotech news in today's Readout newsletter.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about a Merck acquisition, the rise of former Loxo execs at Lilly, and more
summary: Merck intends to buy Terns Pharmaceuticals, the developer of a promising leukemia treatment, for $6.7 billion

title: Merck to buy Terns in $6.7B bet on a ‘differentiated’ leukemia drug
summary: If consummated, the deal would hand Merck a treatment that could challenge Novartis’ fast-selling Scemblix. But the low purchase premium — which one analyst described as a "steal" — may leave room…

title: RA Capital targets China with latest SPAC deal
summary: The prominent investor aims to merge its latest special purpose acquisition company with a Chinese biotech, according to a securities filing.

title: Gilead, with Ouro deal, wagers $1.7B on bispecifics for autoimmune diseases
summary: Acquiring Ouro Medicines, which launched last year, hands Gilead an antibody designed to bind to BCMA and CD3, a pair of immune cell proteins that are popular targets for drugmakers.

title: Immutrin raises $87M to advance drug for progressive heart disease
summary: The fresh funding will help the biotech startup advance a candidate to treat a genetic disorder, “ATTR-CM,” that has become a battleground for companies like Pfizer, Alnylam and BridgeBio.
